title: Footer support action can open the wrong fixed modal

evidence.quote:
<button
  onClick={() => setOpen(true)}
  aria-label={t("button")}
  className="fixed bottom-6 right-6 z-40 flex items-center gap-2 rounded-full border border-white/10 bg-black/80 px-4 py-2.5 text-xs font-medium text-slate-300 shadow-lg hover:border-cyan-400/30 hover:text-cyan-400 transition-all duration-200"

<button
  onClick={() => setOpen(true)}
  aria-label={t("button")}
  className="fixed bottom-6 left-6 z-40 flex items-center gap-2 rounded-full border border-white/10

reasoning: The footer's Support link does not target the support component directly. It clicks the first button in the document that has an aria-label and a className containing fixed. Both the support and feedback floating buttons match that predicate, so DOM order determines whether the Support footer link opens support or feedback. This is a user-visible correctness bug and will be brittle as more fixed aria-label buttons are added.
reproduction: Render a page with both FeedbackButton and SupportButton where FeedbackButton appears earlier in DOM order, then click Footer -> Support. The feedback modal opens instead of the support modal.
recommendation: Replace the DOM query/click coupling with explicit state or a shared support-modal controller/context. At minimum, dispatch a named custom event or query a stable data attribute unique to the support button, not all fixed aria-label buttons.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests were included for footer interactions or coexistence of the floating support and feedback buttons.
suggestedRegressionTest: Render Footer with both floating buttons present, click the localized Support footer item, and assert that SupportModal opens while FeedbackModal remains closed.
minimumFixScope: frontend/src/components/footer.tsx and the component or shared state that owns SupportModal opening.
